# Bitfinex.Net

> Strongly typed .NET client library for the Bitfinex REST and WebSocket APIs. Supports spot trading, margin, derivatives symbols, funding, account management, market data, and order placement. Part of the CryptoExchange.Net ecosystem providing a unified abstraction across cryptocurrency exchanges.

Bitfinex.Net provides strongly typed public and private endpoints, automatic WebSocket reconnection, comprehensive error handling via the `HttpResult<T>` / `WebSocketResult<T>` pattern, native AOT support, local order book support, and SharedApis integration for exchange-agnostic code. Current package version in this repository: 11.2.0. Targets netstandard2.0, netstandard2.1, net8.0, net9.0, net10.0.

REST methods return `HttpResult<T>` / `HttpResult`, WebSocket subscriptions return `WebSocketResult<UpdateSubscription>`, and shared symbol/cache helpers return `ExchangeCallResult<T>`. Call `SharedClient.Discover()` to inspect supported shared features before routing optional multi-exchange functionality.

Shared spot and futures symbol retrieval supports `GetSymbolsRequest` filters and returns display names plus base/quote asset type and subtype metadata. After a successful retrieval, use `ISpotSymbolRestClient.SpotSymbolCatalog` or `IFuturesSymbolRestClient.FuturesSymbolCatalog` for the cached catalog.

## Documentation

- [README](https://github.com/JKorf/Bitfinex.Net/blob/master/README.md): Overview, installation, supported features, basic usage examples, release notes
- [Documentation Site](https://cryptoexchange.jkorf.dev/Bitfinex.Net/): Full documentation hub including client reference, WebSocket subscriptions, dependency injection, and shared interfaces
- [AI API Quick Map](https://github.com/JKorf/Bitfinex.Net/blob/master/docs/ai-api-map.md): Quick intent-to-method map for routing common user requests to the correct client member
- [Full LLM Context](https://github.com/JKorf/Bitfinex.Net/blob/master/llms-full.txt): Detailed AI/LLM guide with code patterns, endpoint map, common mistakes, and anti-hallucination rules

## Examples

- [AI-friendly examples directory](https://github.com/JKorf/Bitfinex.Net/tree/master/Examples/ai-friendly): Compact examples optimized for AI assistants and quick onboarding
- [Spot Quickstart](https://github.com/JKorf/Bitfinex.Net/blob/master/Examples/ai-friendly/01-spot-quickstart.cs): Client setup, ticker, wallets, limit order placement, order status check
- [Margin and Funding](https://github.com/JKorf/Bitfinex.Net/blob/master/Examples/ai-friendly/02-margin-funding.cs): Margin position lookup, funding ticker, and funding offer pattern
- [WebSocket Streams](https://github.com/JKorf/Bitfinex.Net/blob/master/Examples/ai-friendly/03-websocket.cs): Ticker and kline subscriptions with proper teardown
- [Multi-Exchange Pattern](https://github.com/JKorf/Bitfinex.Net/blob/master/Examples/ai-friendly/04-multi-exchange.cs): CryptoExchange.Net SharedApis, capability discovery, and shared subscriptions
- [Error Handling](https://github.com/JKorf/Bitfinex.Net/blob/master/Examples/ai-friendly/05-error-handling.cs): HttpResult, WebSocketResult, and ExchangeCallResult patterns

## Reference

- [CryptoExchange.Net base library](https://github.com/JKorf/CryptoExchange.Net): Base abstractions used by Bitfinex.Net and other exchange libraries
- [CryptoClients.Net](https://github.com/JKorf/CryptoClients.Net): Bundle package providing unified access to exchange libraries
- [NuGet Package](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Bitfinex.Net): Latest stable release on NuGet
- [Discord Community](https://discord.gg/MSpeEtSY8t): Maintainer-supported Discord for questions and discussion
